Package org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor.wizards

Class AbstractEventBCreationWizard

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor.wizards.AbstractEventBCreationWizard
Direct Known Subclasses:
EventBCreationWizards.NewAxiomsWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewCarrierSetsWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewConstantsWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewEnumeratedSetWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewEventsWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewInvariantsWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewVariablesWizard, EventBCreationWizards.NewVariantWizard
public abstract class AbstractEventBCreationWizard extends Object
This class synthesizes the editor wizard to create EventB elements. Subclasses have to override createDialog(IEventBRoot, Shell) method in order to specify the dialog to be displayed. Subclasses have to override getCreationOperation(IEventBRoot, EventBDialog) to handle the information at dialog close and create corresponding EventB elements. The method getAndRegisterCreationOperation(EventBDialog) tries to register the new element if there is any IEventBEditor linked. See also IEventBEditor.addNewElement(org.rodinp.core.IRodinElement) is called.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractEventBCreationWizard

  • Method Details

    • createDialog

      protected abstract EventBDialog createDialog(IEventBRoot root, org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Shell shell)
      Creates a dialog to assist element creation.
      Parameters:
      root - the root element for which elements will be created
      shell - the current shell for the dialog
      Returns:
      the dialog to assist element creation

    • getCreationOperation

      public abstract AtomicOperation getCreationOperation(IEventBRoot root, EventBDialog dialog)
      Method retrieving the informations from the given dialog and creates the intended elements in the given root element.
      Parameters:
      root - the root element for which elements will be created
      dialog - the dialog that originated the element creation
      Returns:
      the atomic operation corresponding to the element creation that has been created

    • openDialog

      public AtomicOperation openDialog(IEventBEditor<?> editor)
      Open a wizard to assist the creation of an EventB element for a given IEventBEditor and handles the element creation by calling back the editor.
      Parameters:
      editor - the IEventBEditor to be called back
      Returns:
      the atomic operation performed

    • openDialog

      public AtomicOperation openDialog(IEventBRoot root, org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Shell shell)
      Open a wizard to assist the creation of a element of a given root for a given undo-redo managing history
      Parameters:
      root - the root element for which elements will be created
      shell - the parent shell
      Returns:
      the atomic operation corresponding to the element creation

    • getAndRegisterCreationOperation

      public AtomicOperation getAndRegisterCreationOperation(EventBDialog dialog)
      Gets the atomic operation corresponding to the element creation and registers it in the history associated with this wizard. It attempts to register the corresponding new element in the current IEventBEditor if it exists.
      Parameters:
      dialog - the dialog requesting the creation operation and having the necessary information to do it
      Returns:
      the created operation

    • addNewElement

      private static void addNewElement(IEventBEditor<?> editor, AtomicOperation op)
      Add the element concerned by the operation to the given editor via the call-back method IEventBEditor.addNewElement(org.rodinp.core.IRodinElement)
      Parameters:
      editor - the editor to add the element to
      op - the corresponding atomic operation performed

    • isCreationAllowed

      public static boolean isCreationAllowed(IEventBRoot root)
      Tells if creation is available for the given root. Event-B roots which are generated or read-only do not allow creation of further element from the UI, and thus creation possibilities shall be disabled.
      Parameters:
      root - the root element for which wizards should allow element creation
      Returns:
      true if the creation is possible, false otherwise.
